How It Relates to the Analysis: Measures the size of a country’s economy. Used to understand whether economic prosperity influences the relationship between inequality and happiness.
Captures national investment in human capital. Helps test whether development conditions moderate the inequality–happiness relationship.
Reflects investment in public health systems. Provides context for well‑being beyond income and inequality.
Indicates macroeconomic stability. High inflation can reduce real income and affect life satisfaction, making it relevant for understanding economic stress.
Represents the share of people living in urban areas. Urbanization influences social structure, access to services, and overall well‑being.
